grants.name: Federal Volunteer Grants 2025–26
grants.notes: ⚠️ 10 DAYS LEFT. EOIs through local Federal MP. $66,665 per electorate. Must have 40%+ volunteer workforce. Both orgs are volunteer-driven — strong fit.

grants.name: Local Sporting Champions (Round 4)
grants.notes: For individual coaches, officials and competitors aged 12–18 attending championships. Could support SuperNova U18 players.

grants.name: Whittlesea Community Grants 2025-26
grants.notes: NEW — North. All year round grants open until 30 April 2026. Strong multicultural community (South Morang, Mernda, Epping, Mill Park). Also has Female Inclusion in Sport Grants (co-funded with DPV Health). Contact community.grants@whittlesea.vic.gov.au or 9217 2170.

grants.name: Whitehorse Community Grants 2026
grants.notes: Eastern suburbs. Mitcham Badminton is in Whitehorse — clean venue fit for SuperNova Learn & Play. Also Partnership Grants up to $40K/yr for 4 years. Contact grants@whitehorse.vic.gov.au.

grants.name: Darebin Community Grants 2026-27
grants.notes: NEW — North. Closes 4 May 2026. Covers Preston, Reservoir, Northcote. Priority groups include CALD, women, young people. Must deliver within Darebin. Contact Darebingrants@darebin.vic.gov.au.

grants.name: City of Melbourne Connected Neighbourhoods
grants.notes: Monthly assessment rounds. Only relevant if TVS delivers programs within City of Melbourne boundaries.

grants.name: City of Melbourne — Town Halls Scheme (CUTHS)
categories: Free venue use for NFP events
grants.notes: In-kind support for NFP organisations to use Melbourne Town Halls at no cost.

grants.name: Wyndham Medium Grants – Round 2
grants.notes: Strongest council opportunity — both orgs are Wyndham-based. Co-contribution required (volunteer hours at $45/hr count). Priority: women, CALD communities.

grants.name: Wyndham Small Grants
grants.notes: Rolling applications. Quick wins for equipment or small program costs. Both orgs Wyndham-based.

grants.name: Hume Sports Aid Grants — Pathway (Batch 2)
grants.notes: NEW — North. For Hume residents aged 24 and under. Financial support for representative competition. Could support SuperNova U18 players who live in Hume. Closes 30 June 2026.

grants.name: Merri-bek Community Participation Grants (Groups)
grants.notes: NEW — North. Rolling quick-response funding for participation projects. Covers Brunswick, Coburg, Fawkner. Must benefit Merri-bek residents. Contact communitygrants@merri-bek.vic.gov.au.

closedGrants.name: Victorian Sporting Club Grants (Round 2)
closedGrants.reason: Closed 7 April 2026. All categories reopen in 2026-27 Round 1 (~Oct 2026).

closedGrants.name: Maroondah Annual Community Grants 2026/27
closedGrants.reason: Closed 2 April 2026. Up to $10K. Outcomes mid-July 2026. Enquiry sent to Josh Burt re: outside-council venue.

closedGrants.name: Darebin Older and Active Community Grant 2026-27
Hume Annual Community Grants 2026 (Round 2)
closedGrants.reason: Round 2 applications closed. 92 recipients shared $352K. Watch for 2027 round.

closedGrants.name: Merri-bek Annual Grants 2026-27
closedGrants.reason: Closed 28 February 2026. Community Strengthening (up to $7K), Facility Improvement, Organisational Establishment (up to $10K/yr for 3 yrs). Next round ~Jan 2027.

closedGrants.name: Hobsons Bay Make it Happen Grants
closedGrants.reason: Closed 30 March 2026. Up to $25K. Next round ~Feb 2027.

closedGrants.name: Melton Community Grants 2025/26
closedGrants.reason: Closed 31 March 2026. Up to $5K. Watch for 2026/27.

closedGrants.name: Knox Leisure Minor Capital Works Grant
closedGrants.reason: Closed 15 March 2026. Opens 1 Feb each year.

closedGrants.name: City of Melbourne Community Inclusion Grants 2026-27
City of Melbourne Community Events Grants 2026-27
Change Our Game Community Activation Grants 2024-25
closedGrants.reason: Closed. Up to $10K for women in sport. Watch for 2025-26 round.

closedGrants.name: Sport & Rec Vic Significant Sporting Events

upcomingGrants.name: City of Melbourne Annual Arts Grants 2027
upcomingGrants.note: Arts projects in City of Melbourne.

upcomingGrants.name: Knox Community Development Fund 2026-27
upcomingGrants.note: Eastern suburbs — ACM Bayswater is in Knox. Sport/leisure, multicultural, community strengthening. Strong fit for SuperNova eastern expansion.

upcomingGrants.name: Hume Community Grants 2027
upcomingGrants.note: North. Broadmeadows, Craigieburn, Sunbury. Strong multicultural community. Operational grants ($1.5K), Community Strengthening ($7K), 3-year grants ($10K/yr). Previously funded multicultural events and women's sport.

upcomingGrants.name: Whittlesea Female Inclusion in Sport Grants
upcomingGrants.note: North. Co-funded with DPV Health. Specifically for women's sport participation. Excellent fit for SuperNova Learn & Play — Women Only.

upcomingGrants.name: Merri-bek Annual Grants 2027-28
upcomingGrants.note: North. Brunswick, Coburg, Fawkner. Priority: migrants, refugees, women, CALD communities.

upcomingGrants.name: Change Our Game Community Activation Grants 2025-26
upcomingGrants.note: Perfect for SuperNova Women's Learn & Play. 3 streams: Participation, Education, Community Leaders.

upcomingGrants.name: Multicultural Festivals & Events Program 2025-26
upcomingGrants.note: Strong fit for TVS multicultural events (Holi, Diwali).

upcomingGrants.name: Wyndham Active Revitalisation Program
upcomingGrants.note: $3.75M program. Requires lease/licence at Council facility.

upcomingGrants.name: Sporting Club Grants Program 2026-27 (Round 1)
upcomingGrants.note: All 5 categories available again for SuperNova.

meta.profile: Early-stage AI software company, under $20M turnover, incorporated company
meta.disclaimer: Programs open and close regularly. Verify all details directly with the relevant agency before applying.

grants.name: R&D Tax Incentive (RDTI)
grants.amount_display: 43.5% refundable tax offset (~$21,750 back on $50K spend)
grants.action: Engage R&D Tax Incentive specialist accountant immediately. Start logging all eligible DealDesk AI development activities from today.
grants.fit_notes: ASR Solutions Pty Ltd is an incorporated company. DealDesk AI involved genuine technical uncertainty in classifier, draft generator, OAuth, and prompt engineering systems.
relevant_activities: Developing novel AI classification prompts where outcome was technically uncertain
Building and testing the Make.com + OpenAI pipeline where behaviour was unknown in advance
Engineering industry-specific guardrail system (no equivalent product existed)
OAuth black box and Upstash AES-256-GCM token management (new technical approach)
Experimental prompt engineering where multiple approaches were tested and failed
warnings: Records must be kept during the year — not reconstructed retrospectively
ATO has tightened audits — document the uncertainty, not just the outcome
Grants received for R&D work reduce claimable RDTI expenditure dollar-for-dollar
Missing registration deadline means no claim for that year — no extensions

grants.name: Industry Growth Program — Stage 1
grants.administered_by: Dept of Industry, Science and Resources
grants.deadline: Rolling — check portal for current round
grants.amount_display: Up to $25,000 in funded advisory support
grants.action: Visit business.gov.au, search Industry Growth Program, check Stage 1 eligibility for early-stage digital economy businesses.
grants.fit_notes: DealDesk AI is in the Digital Economy priority sector. Stage 1 advisory funding is the entry point before Stage 2 commercialisation grants.
relevant_activities: Stage 1 can fund a commercialisation advisor, grant writer, or growth strategist
Reducing the cost of scaling DealDesk AI to market

grants.name: ASBAS Digital Solutions — Round 3
grants.administered_by: Dept of Industry, Science and Resources
grants.action: Check business.gov.au for ASBAS Digital Solutions Round 3. Use as a client onboarding support tool for mortgage brokers adopting DealDesk AI.
grants.fit_notes: More relevant as a client enablement tool than direct FifthArk funding. Brokers and agents can access subsidised advisory to adopt DealDesk AI.
relevant_activities: FifthArk clients (mortgage brokers, agents) accessing subsidised advisory
Useful as a client acquisition pathway and sales talking point

grants.name: LaunchVic — Victorian AI and DeepTech Programs
grants.deadline: Subscribe to alerts — rounds close fast
grants.amount_display: Varies — up to $400,000 in recent rounds
grants.action: Register on launchvic.org immediately. Subscribe to grant alerts. Apply at first opportunity when AI-specific founder round opens.
grants.fit_notes: FifthArk is a Melbourne-based AI company — exactly LaunchVic's target. Location, stage, and sector all align. Timing-dependent.
relevant_activities: AI-specific founder rounds open periodically
Victorian-based founders in DeepTech and AI are priority

grants.name: City of Melbourne Business Growth and Impact Grants
grants.action: Verify FifthArk's registered address is within City of Melbourne municipality. Monitor for next round opening.
grants.fit_notes: Address eligibility check required. DealDesk AI as a new product launch is a strong framing. Apply in first week of opening — competitive.
relevant_activities: DealDesk AI qualifies as new product launch under Expansion stream
Must operate within City of Melbourne municipality boundaries
warnings: Address eligibility must be confirmed before applying

grants.name: Victorian Small Business Digital Adaptation Program
grants.deadline: Check portal — previous round closed 2021, new rounds may have launched
grants.action: Search business.vic.gov.au for 'Technology Adoption and Innovation Program' and 'Digital Adaptation Program' for current open rounds.
grants.fit_notes: Availability uncertain. Most useful if a new round specifically targets AI adoption. Set quarterly calendar reminder to check.
relevant_activities: FifthArk clients adopting DealDesk AI could access this
Relevant if FifthArk is investing in its own digital infrastructure

grants.name: Export Market Development Grant (EMDG)
grants.deadline: Annual application cycle — not relevant until first international client
grants.amount_display: Up to 50% of export expenses, up to $770,000 lifetime
grants.action: Not relevant yet. Start tracking export-eligible expenses once first international client is signed. Target: NZ, UK, Canada.
grants.fit_notes: High value over time. Natural expansion markets: NZ (similar regulatory environment), UK, Canada — all have mortgage brokers and migration agents.
relevant_activities: International marketing and conferences
Overseas client acquisition costs
Export market feasibility studies

grants.name: Cooperative Research Centres Projects (CRC-P) — AI Round
grants.administered_by: Dept of Industry, Science and Resources
grants.deadline: Bi-annual rounds — Round 19 closed May 2026, watch for Round 20
grants.amount_display: Collaborative research funding up to 3 years
grants.action: Develop university research partnerships. Target: Monash Business School or RMIT for professional services AI research. Apply in Round 20.
grants.fit_notes: High potential if university partnership established. Good fit with National AI Plan priority. Requires significant relationship investment first.
relevant_activities: AI for professional services research with Monash or RMIT
National AI Plan priority area alignment
warnings: Must partner with an Australian university or CSIRO — relationship takes time to build

strategy.action: Engage R&D Tax Incentive specialist accountant
strategy.why: Highest return, always open, requires contemporaneous records — start logging now

strategy.action: Check Industry Growth Program Stage 1 eligibility and apply
strategy.why: Up to $25K funded advisory — use to fund grant writer or commercialisation specialist

strategy.action: Register on LaunchVic and subscribe to grant alerts
strategy.why: AI-specific rounds open periodically — must be registered to know when they open

strategy.action: Verify City of Melbourne municipality eligibility
strategy.why: $15-20K available if address qualifies. Monitor for next round late 2026.

strategy.action: Monitor Business Victoria for new digital adoption rounds
strategy.why: Periodic rounds — set a calendar reminder to check quarterly

strategy.action: Track export activity for EMDG eligibility
strategy.why: Up to $770K lifetime — becomes relevant at first international client
